title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After a man helps Joyce clean a wound on her wrist caused by her dog, Tracy, he departs. Mrs. Barnes arrives to offer Joyce tea and comfort, suggesting that Joyce's husband will provide a new dog if Tracy does not recover. However, Joyce receives a phone call informing her that Tracy has died alone.

Joyce’s husband, Arthur (referred to as Harrydy), arrives and attempts to take Joyce away from her current home. Joyce informs him that she will not marry him because Tracy is dead, revealing that her motive for the marriage was merely to be with the dog. She expresses her deep hatred for Arthur, describing him as a cruel person who enjoys hurting others. After Joyce rejects his offer of financial stability, an angry Arthur leaves.

Feeling numb and contemplating suicide, Joyce visits an employment agency. Mrs. Lambert informs her that no local daytime jobs are available, so Joyce agrees to consider working abroad. She is interviewed by Mr. Alabi, who offers her a position caring for his elderly aunt and his three-year-old son. During the interview, Mr. Alabi asks about her dog. Joyce realizes that he is the same gentleman who helped her earlier. He shares that he also lost a dog two years ago, which creates a moment of profound emotional connection between them. Joyce accepts the job and signs the necessary documents. Upon returning home, Mrs. Barnes informs Joyce that Tracy's body has been delivered and that Barnes will dig a pit in the backyard for the dog's burial.
